category_title: Young children with persistent challenging behaviors across multiple routines
minimal_context: Kayden is at home and is being prompted to start getting ready for bed.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Kayden, it is time to start getting ready for bed.

STUDENT: (Starts crying)

TUTOR: Let's go get your pajamas.

STUDENT: (Screams)

TUTOR: We have to get ready for bed now.

STUDENT: (Screams and begins hitting)

options.A: Covertly listen to and watch Kayden during the bedtime routine, but never engage directly; intervene only if his behaviors are extremely loud or disruptive, or if he asks for help with conflict resolution.
options.B: Ascertain topics Kayden cares about and make available diverse genres of bedtime books on relevant topics (e.g., picture books, pop-up books, fairy tales) to use consistently throughout the nightly routine.
options.C: Incorporate opportunities to give attention to Kayden during appropriate activities that occur just before bedtime, focusing on positive interactions in mutually enjoyable activities such as playing low-energy games, working on a puzzle, or singing a silly song.
options.D: Implement the use of a speech-generating device to enable Kayden to 'read aloud' from his favorite book when called on during the bedtime routine, incorporating this technology into the nightly schedule.
